channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: What we know about the deadly Christmas market attack in Germany
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Four women and a 9-year-old boy were killed and 200 people were injured when a man drove into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers in the German city of Magdeburg — an attack that has left Germans mourning the victims and with a shaken sense of security.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ukrainian drones hit a Russian fuel depot for the second time this month
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukrainian drones struck a major Russian fuel depot for the second time in just over a week on Sunday, according to a senior Russian regional official, as part of a “massive” cross-border attack on fuel and energy facilities that Kyiv says supply Moscow's military.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — The Chinese government protested Sunday the latest American announcements of military sales and assistance to Taiwan, warning the United States that it is “playing with fire.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: German Christmas market attack suspect in custody under murder charges
item.description: The man suspected of driving a car through crowds at a German Christmas market is in custody on murder charges, police said Sunday. On Friday, Taleb Al Abdulmohsen, a resident of Germany originally from Saudi Arabia, allegedly rammed a car into a busy holiday market in the German city of Magdeburg.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Prominent Lebanese figure meets Syrian insurgent who led Assad's ouster, seeking better relations
item.description: A prominent Lebanese politician has met with the insurgent who led the overthrow of Syrian leader Bashar Assad, and both expressed hope Sunday for a new era in relations between their countries.
item.dc:creator: Sally Abou Aljoud, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Stampedes in Nigeria leave at least 32 dead during Christmas food distribution
item.description: Nigerian police say the death toll from stampedes during two Christmas charity events has increased from 13 to 32.
item.dc:creator: Dyepkazah Shibayan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 2 US Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident, US military says
item.description: The U.S. military says two U.S. Navy pilots have been shot down over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The water was their livelihood. Now Thailand's sea nomads work to preserve a vanishing way of life
item.description: SURIN ISLANDS, Thailand (AP) — When Hook was a child, he started his days by jumping off the boat that his family lived on and into the ocean. By age 3, he could already swim and dive in shallow waters. His home was a kabang, a boat, that his family sailed in Thailand’s southern waters.
item.dc:creator: HUIZHONG WU and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US military conducts successful airstrikes on Houthi rebel forces in Yemen
item.description: The U.S. military on Saturday said it conducted precision airstrikes on a missile storage site and a command-and-control center in Yemen against Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: Albanian authorities held 1,300 meetings with teachers and parents following the stabbing death of a teenager in mid-November by another teen after a quarrel that started on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: Llazar Semini, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — One week after its worst cyclone in nearly a century, and a day after a testy presidential visit, France’s impoverished Indian Ocean territory of Mayotte is still grappling with counting the dead, restoring essential services and aiding a beleaguered population.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, WGN 9

item.title: Projectile from Yemen strikes near Tel Aviv, injuring more than a dozen: officials
item.description: A projectile launched into Israel from Yemen overnight struck Tel Aviv, leading to 16 people suffering mild injuries, according to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia agrees to resume 'no find, no fee' hunt for flight MH370, 10 years after plane disappeared
item.description: Over 10 years after flight MH370 is believed to have crashed in the Indian Ocean, Malaysia's government has agreed to accept a "no find, no fee" proposal from a U.S. company to resume the hunt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden missing in action as Turkey inches closer to full-blown war against US-allied Kurds in Syria
item.description: Amid the end of the nearly 14 years of civil war in Syria, Turkish strongman Recep Tayyip Erdoğan is set to rekindle a new war in northern Syria against America’s main ally, the Syrian Kurds.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US officials travel to Syria in search of missing Americans, meet with rebel leaders
item.description: Top U.S. officials are in Syria making the first face-to-face contact with rebel groups that took over the country in a lightning offensive earlier this month, their mission aimed at searching for kidnapped American journalist Austin Tice and other missing Americans.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Italian artisans make around 100K bulbs for the signature light display at Singapore's Christmas Wonderland
item.description: Christmas Wonderland is a festive event that happens annually in Singapore. The festival is complete with breathtaking illuminations, activities, food and more.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP seeks ‘maximum pressure’ on Iran with nuclear clock ticking
item.description: Republican lawmakers want a return to "maximum pressure" on Iran in the second Trump administration and are widely open to the idea of strikes on Iranian nuclear sites if it’s the only way to prevent Tehran from wielding nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US diplomats in Damascus for first time in more than 10 years following fall of Assad regime
item.description: U.S. officials are in Syria with goals to advance Aqaba principles and seek information on American citizens who disappeared under the Assad regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pentagon doubled the number of US troops in Syria 'months' ahead of Assad’s fall
item.description: The U.S. for months has had more than 2,000 troops inside Syria, more than double the 900 troops the Defense Department previously said are on the ground there, the Pentagon’s top spokesperson revealed Thursday. The increased force presence has “been going on for a while,” with press secretary Maj.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Pro-Palestinian activists storm basketball court during Israeli team’s game against France
item.description: Anti-Israel agitators disrupted an Israeli-French basketball game Wednesday, waving Palestinian flags and sparking chaos in the stands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia says it has detained a suspect in the Moscow bombing that killed a senior general
item.description: Russia's security service said Wednesday it detained a citizen from Uzbekistan in the bombing that killed a senior general as he left his southeastern Moscow apartment — a bold assassination that was claimed by Ukraine's security service. Lt. Gen.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cartel-run clandestine fentanyl labs increasingly difficult to track
item.description: Mexican drug cartels are driving an opioid epidemic, flooding the United States with increasingly potent synthetic drugs.
item.dc:creator: Jorge Ventura, NewsNation
